story.text: As bdo, the experience director's Pattern Commons — the governed vocabulary of UX patterns I praise and refuse into existence — had evolved off in the foundry and needed a real home in the repo, governed, with a portfolio I can actually look at. This atom re-homes that work: the evolved Pattern Commons v1 landed at causality/patterns/ as governed vocabulary (families, rubric, deposits, reconciliation) plus a playable portfolio, with a merge.py that reproduces v1 byte-identical so the commons is a fold, not a hand-kept file. It advances epic.causality-surface (the report reads it that way) and stays PROPOSED — promoting the Commons past proposed is mine (D-4).

briefing.headline: Home the evolved Pattern Commons v1 at causality/patterns/: governed vocabulary + a reproducible merge + a playable portfolio
briefing.value: claude/home-patterns homes the evolved Pattern Commons (v1) into causality/patterns/ (23 files): the commons itself (pattern-commons.v1.json, current-commons.json, ten evolved family files under families/), its governance (CLAUDE.md, RUBRIC.md, DEPOSITS.md, ISSUES.md, RECONCILIATION.md, LOOP.md, PORTFOLIO-DONE.md, LAB-PARKED.md), a merge.py that reproduces v1 byte-identical (the commons is a fold over its families, not a hand-kept artifact), and portfolio.html — a playable portfolio of the patterns. The handoff report 0071 reads it as epic.causality-surface and records v1 as PROPOSED (promotion is bdo's), the C19·RegisterFacet candidate held out of display-system.md until stamped, and the parked Theme-A / variation-lab threads.
briefing.why_now: The experience work kept being hard to judge because the Commons lived in the foundry, ungoverned and unviewable. Homing it at causality/patterns/ with a reproducing merge and a playable portfolio makes it a governed, inspectable surface — the Pattern Commons is what the experience skill consults first, so it has to be real in the repo, not a session's memory.
briefing.if_accepted: The evolved Pattern Commons is governed and homed in the repo, reproducible byte-identical by merge.py, with a portfolio bdo can open and judge — the experience skill consults a real commons, not foundry state. v1 stays PROPOSED until bdo promotes it.
briefing.if_rejected: The Commons stays in the foundry, off the log and ungoverned; the portfolio is unviewable, the family vocabulary undeposited, and the experience work keeps being judged against a commons that isn't in the repo.
briefing.cost_of_wrong_call: Low and reversible: the content is governed vocabulary (markdown + JSON), a deterministic merge.py fold, and a static portfolio.html (no runtime network, nothing written to the truth log); merge.py reproduces v1 byte-identical so the commons cannot silently drift, and reverting is reverting one PR. It mints nothing — v1 is PROPOSED (D-4).
briefing.mechanism: causality/patterns/: pattern-commons.v1.json + families/*.evolved.json (the vocabulary), merge.py (reproduces v1 byte-identical from the families — the commons as a fold), the governance markdowns (RUBRIC/DEPOSITS/ISSUES/RECONCILIATION/LOOP/PORTFOLIO-DONE/LAB-PARKED + CLAUDE.md), and portfolio.html (the playable portfolio). 803 green at the report. Report 0071 carries the handoff and the parked threads.

must_not_collide_with: causality/display-system.md (the C19·RegisterFacet candidate is held out until bdo stamps; this homes the commons, it does not write the new facet into the display system)

_note: Re-homing atom for claude/home-patterns (the evolved Pattern Commons v1), authored so the off-log work carries an atom on the log under the atom-invariant gate. v1 is PROPOSED — homing it mints nothing; promotion is bdo's (D-4). Judged by an independent value-gate (D-2). Never mutated; verdicts are the fold over .ai-native/log/ (D-5).
